structural_guidance: The centaur chess explanation can be shortened — the concept is clear, the origin story does not need as much space

Lin and Sohail and Cotton and Scholle-Cotton quotes can be paraphrased more tightly rather than set up with full attribution sentences each time — but preserve the key phrases (expertise erosion paradox, short-circuiting the apprenticeship)

The skill atrophy analogies (language, instrument, cycling) — all three make the same point. Keep two, cut one. The language and cycling analogies are strongest (language for universality, cycling for the baseline vs high-performance distinction)
The complacency cascade section and distorted signals section can be merged into a single tighter subsection on population-level effects
The five training recommendations are verbose — tighten each to 1-2 sentences while preserving all five points

The long-term role section (autonomous labs, solve disease X, mountain-climbing analogy) should be kept but compressed — preserve the reference to a future post and the key examples (disease X trade-offs, mountain-climbing analogy) but tighten the surrounding prose
The strong/weak bundle paragraph in The two sides together should be preserved — it is a recent addition and well-integrated
Preserve all MARGIN comments — they are part of the post format
Preserve all cross-reference links to companion posts
